purpose: Convert production reference decompositions into hard native-PPT composition, typography, spacing, and sequence contracts for the next rerun.

moves.composition_contract: Use one focal native canvas field that occupies at least 42 percent of the slide; the cover must not expose report panels before the product promise lands.
moves.typography_contract: Use one headline hierarchy with a 3x title-to-label size gap and no more than two secondary labels.
moves.spacing_contract: Keep wide whitespace around the focal field, preserve quiet outer margins, and avoid filling margin space with badges.
moves.provenance_boundary: Use derived observations only; do not copy screenshots, brand marks, source layouts, source prose, video frames, audio, or transcripts.
code_generation_contract: Generate a native PPT module, not a screenshot or full-slide raster.
Use editable headline text, grouped native shapes, and a traceable opening sequence.
Record fallback state if the focal object cannot be built natively.
forbidden_report_patterns: report header with no focal object
moves.qa_probe: The contact sheet should read as a cinematic product opening, not an experiment report cover.

moves.composition_contract: Use an asymmetric canvas with before and after states; the after state must have the focal object and the before state must look intentionally weaker.
moves.typography_contract: Use headline hierarchy for state labels, one delta label, and one short implication line.
moves.spacing_contract: Use whitespace to separate states and keep the margin around the after-state field larger than internal card gutters.
moves.provenance_boundary: Use derived observations only; do not copy screenshots, brand marks, source layouts, source prose, video frames, audio, or transcripts.
code_generation_contract: Generate a native PPT module with editable before/after thumbnails and one delta object.
Use native rectangles, lines, connectors, labels, and grouped objects.
Record fallback state if text length forces the after-state object below the required scale.
forbidden_report_patterns: report cards with only text labels
moves.qa_probe: The contact sheet should show a visible before/after transformation, not just text saying transformation.

moves.composition_contract: Use the canvas to choreograph data to memory to generated slide; the generated native slide preview must be the focal object.
moves.typography_contract: Use headline hierarchy for the proof claim and concise functional labels for route nodes.
moves.spacing_contract: Keep whitespace between data, memory, and output zones; preserve a generous margin around the generated slide preview.
moves.provenance_boundary: Use derived observations only; do not copy screenshots, brand marks, source layouts, source prose, video frames, audio, or transcripts.
code_generation_contract: Generate a native PPT module with a proof route and editable mini-preview.
Use sequence metadata to show build order without relying on rendered video.
Record fallback state if the route becomes equal-weight process boxes.
moves.qa_probe: The contact sheet should show a proof route with a generated slide target, not generic workflow cards.

moves.composition_contract: Use a native canvas preview of the product's intended slide output as the focal object; controls must support the preview instead of replacing it.
moves.typography_contract: Use headline hierarchy for the claim and short control labels inside or around the preview.
moves.spacing_contract: Keep preview margin large enough that it reads as a designed object, with whitespace separating controls from the preview frame.
moves.provenance_boundary: Use derived observations only; do not copy screenshots, brand marks, source layouts, source prose, video frames, audio, or transcripts.
code_generation_contract: Generate a native PPT module with editable slide-frame, labels, density controls, and selected memory markers.
Never satisfy the preview with a pasted screenshot.
Record fallback state when the preview cannot remain readable.
moves.qa_probe: The contact sheet should show a native system mini-preview that feels like product behavior, not an explanation box.

moves.composition_contract: Break the prior canvas rhythm and give one focal outcome object at least 48 percent of the slide area.
moves.typography_contract: Use headline hierarchy with one short verdict line and sparse labels around the focal object.
moves.spacing_contract: Use whitespace and wide margin around the hero object so scale feels intentional rather than crowded.
moves.provenance_boundary: Use derived observations only; do not copy screenshots, brand marks, source layouts, source prose, video frames, audio, or transcripts.
code_generation_contract: Generate a native PPT module with an editable hero object, scale cue, and sparse comparison labels.
Use native grouped objects or editable SVG; do not flatten the climax.
Record fallback state if the slide repeats the proof grid.
moves.qa_probe: The contact sheet should make the climax visibly larger and more dramatic than proof slides.

moves.composition_contract: Use a calm decision-handoff canvas with one focal release gate, one next action, and one small native mark.
moves.typography_contract: Use headline hierarchy for the gate status and a smaller support line for the next action.
moves.spacing_contract: Keep wide whitespace and disciplined margin; do not convert the close into an appendix grid.
moves.provenance_boundary: Use derived observations only; do not copy screenshots, brand marks, source layouts, source prose, video frames, audio, or transcripts.
code_generation_contract: Generate a native PPT module with editable release gate and handoff line.
Keep all status text native and trace the public-blocked reason.
Record fallback state if the close becomes a report summary.
moves.qa_probe: The contact sheet should close on a clear decision handoff, not another evidence page.
